message:
feat(pager,wal): epoch pipelining eliminates group commit flushing_wait bottleneck

Root cause: threads arriving during FLUSHING phase blocked on condvar
(1-2.7ms avg) while actual WAL I/O was only 150µs — a 10-20x overhead.
This was the dominant bottleneck at 16 threads.

Solution: Epoch pipelining — accept batch submissions during FLUSHING
phase by queuing them for the next epoch instead of blocking.

Key changes:

GroupCommitConsolidator (group_commit.rs):
- Add next_epoch_batches/next_epoch_frame_count fields
- submit_batch() now accepts during FLUSHING → queues for next epoch
- complete_flush() returns bool: true if pipelined batches were promoted
  to pending_batches (caller must flush again)
- abort_flush() also promotes pipelined batches for retry

Pager (pager.rs):
- Remove flushing_wait condvar loop entirely
- Flusher path uses 'flusher_loop: when complete_flush() returns
  promoted=true, loops to flush promoted batches immediately

Results (16-thread persistent WAL):
- Before: 1.0-1.4 Kelem/s, flushing_wait=1-2.7ms
- After:  1.4-1.7 Kelem/s, flushing_wait=0µs
- 43-77% throughput improvement (criterion p < 0.05)
- vs C SQLite: gap narrowed from 0.67x to 0.85x

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
